# double-meh-sw ‚Äî full reference

> The service-worker sibling of double-meh. Browser service workers are the product; zero runtime dependencies; ESM-only. Node >= 18 / Bun / Deno run the test suite through injected `scope`, `fetch`, and `caches`. Design record: double-meh's dev-docs/design.md ¬ß Service Worker (division of labor, meta channels, client-wins, the no-op-degradable invariant).

## Design invariants

- **No-op-degradable**: cache, coalescing, and bundling are performance transforms. Any of them ‚Äî or the whole worker ‚Äî may vanish (first visit, hard reload, unregistration) and the app must be logically indistinguishable. Every failure path degrades to a direct fetch.
- **Client-wins**: a page that announces a library via `io:hello {library}` owns its own bundling; the SW never bundles that client's requests.
- **Synchronous respondWith gating**: only the configured matcher decides whether the SW handles a request; all async work happens after the decision.
- **Meta channels**: `x-io-*` request headers are the per-request data plane (always stripped before the network); postMessage/BroadcastChannel is the control plane.

## Module: double-meh-sw/sw.js

### install(options?) ‚Üí {cacheTier, coalescer, bundle, hub}

Wires `fetch`, `message`, and `activate` listeners on the scope.

Options (`InstallOptions`):
- `scope` ‚Äî ServiceWorkerGlobalScope-like; injectable for tests. Default `globalThis`.
- `version` ‚Äî reported over `io:version` and in the `io:hello` reply.
- `match` ‚Äî string prefix | RegExp | predicate over URLs: which GETs the SW handles. Default: same-origin, non-navigation.
- `cache` ‚Äî `CacheTierOptions` (below).
- `bundler` ‚Äî `BundleWindowOptions` + `match` for eligibility. Absent = no SW bundling.
- `fetch` ‚Äî upstream fetch, injectable. Default `globalThis.fetch`.
- `claim` ‚Äî `clients.claim()` on activate. Default true.

Serve order: cache tier hit ‚Üí bundle window (eligible: matched, not the bundler URL, not a bundle-MIME accept, client not library-owned) ‚Üí coalesced network (+ `maybeStore` predicate). Non-GETs and unmatched requests are never touched.

## Module: double-meh-sw/cache-tier.js

### createCacheTier({cacheName = 'io-shared', caches?, store?}) ‚Üí CacheTier

- `handleFetch(request)` ‚Äî Cache API match; `undefined` = pass; requests with `x-io-no-cache` always pass.
- `put(target, response)` ‚Äî stores 2xx only; the writers are the bundle unpack, the io:fetch transport, and page-side write-through (pages share the same Cache API).
- `maybeStore(request, response)` ‚Äî network responses store only through the `store` predicate (serve-first tier by default).
- `invalidate(pattern)` ‚Äî string prefix | RegExp | predicate over URLs; returns the eviction count. Feature-detects Deno's Cache API (no `keys()`): falls back to exact-URL deletion there ‚Äî a test-runtime accommodation, browsers have `keys()`.

## Module: double-meh-sw/coalesce.js

### createCoalescer() ‚Üí {run, inFlight}

`run(request, fn)`: keys on method + url + accept; concurrent callers share one upstream call and each receives `response.clone()`. Because every tab's traffic passes through the one SW, this is cross-tab dedup ‚Äî something no page-side library can do.

## Module: double-meh-sw/bundle-window.js

### createBundleWindow({url, fetch?, waitTime = 10, maxSize = 20, minSize = 2, onPart?}) ‚Üí {intake, flush, pending}

Transparent bundling for pages without double-meh: `intake(request)` parks eligible GETs; the window flushes on `waitTime` or `maxSize`; one PUT (`{v: 1, parts: [{id, url, method, headers}]}`, content type `application/vnd.double-meh.bundle-request+json`) goes to the bundler; parts settle waiters by id. Degradations ‚Äî all to direct fetches: a window below `minSize`; a failed or non-bundle bundler response (whole window); an omitted part (that waiter only). `onPart(part, response)` observes every non-synthetic part ‚Äî `install()` uses it to write parts through to the cache tier.

## Module: double-meh-sw/messages.js

### createMessageHub({version, cacheTier, scope, capabilities?, channelName = 'io', fetch?}) ‚Üí {handleMessage, isLibraryClient, clients}

`handleMessage(event)` ‚Äî returns a promise for async messages (waitUntil it):
- `io:hello {library?}` ‚Äî records library clients (client-wins); replies `{type, v: CONTRACT_VERSION, version, capabilities}`.
- `io:invalidate {pattern}` ‚Äî evicts from the tier, broadcasts `io:invalidated {pattern, evicted}` on BroadcastChannel `channelName`, replies the same.
- `io:version` ‚Äî replies `{current: version}`.
- `io:upgrade` ‚Äî calls `scope.skipWaiting()`, replies `{done: true}`.
- `io:fetch {id, url, method?, headers?}` + MessageChannel port ‚Äî fetches upstream (under waitUntil via install), seeds GETs into the cache tier, replies on the port `{type: 'io:result', id, status, statusText, headers, body}` with the body as a **transferred** ArrayBuffer; failures reply `{id, error}`. This is the navigation-surviving prefetch: the SW completes the request even if the page goes away, and the next page finds it in the tier.

## Module: double-meh-sw/contract.js

`CONTRACT_VERSION` (1), `MESSAGES`, `CHANNEL` ('io'), `CAPABILITIES`, `ENRICHMENT_PREFIX` ('x-io-'), `matches(match, url)`, `stripEnrichment(request)`.

## Module: double-meh-sw/wire.js

The bundle wire format v1 (shared with double-meh and double-meh-bundler; normative spec in the double-meh design record): `REQUEST_MIME`, `BUNDLE_MIME`, `buildDoc(entries)`, `toResponse(part)` (inline JSON / text / base64; 204 and 304 bodyless), `isBundlePayload(response)`.
